methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Seed → next-stage step-up multiplier (2.5x) to $2M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Robotics & Manufacturing (found 0 same-stage, 1 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 12 same-sector peers exist in Robotics & Manufacturing, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

private.name: Foundation Robotics
private.rationale: Same sector (Robotics & Manufacturing), post-money $3B+ (reported, raise in progress)

data.narrative:
Orbital Composites is a seed-stage robotics and manufacturing company founded by Cole Nielsen that has raised $6.6M to date. The valuation analysis draws on a comp universe of nine private robotics firms (including 1X Technologies, Foundation Robotics, Divergent, Exotec, and Locus Robotics) and one public comparable, though direct peer matching at the seed stage proved limited.

A stage-progression methodology was the primary applicable valuation approach. Using total capital raised of $6.6M as a proxy for enterprise value ($2M base), a 2.5x step-up multiplier typical of seed-to-next-stage progressions yields an implied valuation of $6M. A peer-median approach could not be reliably applied: while 12 same-sector peers exist in robotics and manufacturing, the narrow filters for stage and valuation tier produced fewer than three datapoints, making sector-wide median reliance analytically weak.

The triangulation supports a valuation range of $5M to $7M, with a median of $6M. This narrow range reflects the limited methodological foundation—reliance on a single applicable method rather than multiple independent triangulation points. Key uncertainties include the representativeness of the stage-progression multiplier for this subsector, the degree of technological or market differentiation relative to identified peers, and capital efficiency relative to comparably-staged competitors. Additional market intelligence on recent seed and Series A outcomes in precision robotics would reduce this range.
